content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration · · · Executive/Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                322-5901              322-6441              322-6444

August 31, 1977

Judge Robert K. Puglia
Court of Appeal
Third Appellate District
State Library and Courts Building
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Judge Puglia:

This letter is to confirm our conversation of
August 29, 1977. You asked whether any reporting requirements pursuant to the Political Reform Act arose out of your
attendance at a workshop conducted by the California Academy
of Appellate Lawyers. It is my understanding that all transportation, food and accommodation expenses for you and your
wife for the three days of the workshop were paid by the
Academy.

The Commission provided in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18728 that an official need not disclose payments for intrastate transportation or necessary accommodations if such
payments are provided in connection with a speaking engagement,
seminar or panel, or similar event at which the official
performs speaking or similar services. Accordingly, your
pro rata share of transportation and accommodation expenses
need not be reported on your Statement of Economic Interests.
However, your wife's share of transportation and accommodation
expenses must be reported on your statement.

Please find enclosed a copy of Schedule 721-EE for
disclosing honoraria. You should complete this schedule in
full including the amended material. The statement should
be dated and signed under penalty of perjury that to the
best of your knowledge all of the information contained on
the form is true. The statement then should be filed with
the clerk of the court.

Judge Robert K. Puglia
August 31, 1977
Page Two


If you have any further questions, please do not
hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Ted Prim
Staff Counsel

TP:plh
Enclosure

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

Court of Appeal

THIRD APPELLATE DISTRICT
STATE LIBRARY AND COURTS BUILDING
SACRAMENTO 95814

ROBERT K. PUGLIA
PRESIDING JUSTICE

August 8, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Gentlemen:

On June 4 through 6, 1976, my wife and I were guests of the
California Academy of Appellate Lawyers at the Ojai Valley
Inn, Ojai, California. The Academy is a professional
organization consisting of some of the leading appellate
lawyers in California. The meeting to which my wife and I
were invited is, I understand, an annual affair. A workshop
was conducted all day Saturday and on Sunday morning in which
problems common to the appellate courts and counsel were
discussed. A participant in that workshop and that was, of
course, the reason I was invited. There were social get-
togethers on Friday and Saturday evenings. The Academy paid
for all meals, lodging and transportation from Sacramento to
Ojai and return for both my wife and me.

At the time I filed my statement of economic interests on
February 25, 1977, I gave no thought whether the Ojai meeting
fell within my reporting responsibilities under the Fair
Political Practices Act. It has since occurred to me that
it might be reportable but I confess to a great deal of
uncertainty on the question. Would you be so kind as to
advise me of your opinion of my obligation and if reporting
is required, how it should be accomplished?

Very truly yours,

ROBERT K. PUGLIA

sections.question: You ask so whether any reporting requirements pursuant to the Political Reform Act arose out of your attendance at a workshop conducted by the California Academy of Appellate Lawyers.
sections.conclusion: Accordingly, your pro rata share of transportation and accommodation expenses need not be reported on your Statement of Economic Interests. However, your wife's share of transportation and accommodation expenses must be reported on your statement.
sections.question_synthetic: Judge Puglia inquired whether attendance at a California Academy of Appellate Lawyers workshop, where the Academy paid all transportation, meals, and lodging for him and his wife, triggered reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under California Administrative Code Section 18723, the judge's share of intrastate transportation and accommodation expenses paid in connection with a speaking engagement or panel need not be disclosed; however, his wife's share must be reported on his Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (from Judge Puglia dated August 9, 1977) and FPPC response letter (dated August 31, 1977). Extracted question and conclusion from the FPPC response letter. Minor OCR errors noted in document text (e.g., 'Err Polttzcal' for 'Fair Political', 'rolirlcal' for 'Political'). Reference to California Administrative Code Section 18723 provides legal basis for conclusion.

QUESTION: You ask so whether any reporting requirements pursuant to the Political Reform Act arose out of your attendance at a workshop conducted by the California Academy of Appellate Lawyers.

CONCLUSION: Accordingly, your pro rata share of transportation and accommodation expenses need not be reported on your Statement of Economic Interests. However, your wife's share of transportation and accommodation expenses must be reported on your statement.

embedding.summary: FPPC staff counsel responds to Judge Puglia's inquiry regarding reporting obligations for expenses incurred during a workshop at the California Academy of Appellate Lawyers, clarifying that his expenses are exempt from disclosure but his wife's expenses must be reported.
